justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Jurisdiction-specific rules identified for all 3 jurisdictions — California §226/§351 wage statement and tip protection, Germany BetrVG §87 works council co-determination, Indonesia contractor classification with no legal transparency mandate. (2) Germany works council approval identified as a hard blocking pre-condition (BR-3, AC-3). (3) Driver classification data model difference explicitly identified — employee (CA) vs contractor (ID) with distinct language, fields, and legal obligations (BR-5, AC-5, edge case for relocation). (4) Formal escalation for legal review with structured options and recommendations across 4 escalation items. (5) No single global acceptance criteria set — ACs are jurisdiction-specific throughout. (6) confidence.level = 45, well below the 60 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on California Labor Code §226 and §351, citing specific statutory penalty amounts and class-action exposure.
Germany Betriebsrat requirement correctly identified as a hard gate with system-enforced prevention (AC-3), not just a process note. Also caught the revocation edge case.
Driver classification difference treated as a first-class concern with concrete implications: language differences (service fee vs wage), reclassification risk, and a relocation edge case.
Identified the subtle trap that removing transparency in California would be illegal even if simplifying it in Indonesia is fine — framed through invariant vs non-invariant classification.
Business impact correctly identifies that global rollout cannot be uniform, with phased jurisdiction-specific approach recommended via escalation Option A for Germany.
Confidence at 45 with well-articulated low-confidence areas including whether Indonesian regulatory landscape has changed — showing appropriate humility about legal complexity.
Edge cases are strong — tip adjustments, delivery reversals, cumulative deduction minimum wage breach, and the cross-jurisdiction relocation scenario all demonstrate deep domain thinking.

Four structured escalations with options and recommendations, covering deductions definition, real-time definition, legal review mandate, and Betriebsrat timeline — all formally escalated with affected stakeholders.

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
